title: Tamil Urulai Roast

ingredients.name: baby potatoes or small potatoes, halved

subs.name: raw banana (vazhakkai), peeled and cubed — more traditional and slightly firmer
cumin seeds — the flavour note shifts but the texture is the same

ingredients.name: gingelly oil
visual_checks: or regular oil — gingelly adds a characteristic nuttiness
ingredients.notes: or regular oil — gingelly adds a characteristic nuttiness

steps.text: Parboil the potatoes in salted water for 8 minutes until a knife slides in with a little resistance — they should be about 70% cooked, not fully done. Drain well and pat dry with a cloth or paper; any surface moisture prevents crisping.
Heat gingelly oil in a kadai on medium flame. Add fennel seeds and let them sizzle for 20 seconds. Add the shallots and curry leaves and fry until the shallots are golden, about 4 minutes.
Reduce to low-medium flame. Add the parboiled potatoes and toss in the oil. Add red chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric, and salt. Toss well so every piece is coated.

Cook on medium-low heat for 15 minutes, turning the potatoes every 3–4 minutes, until each piece is deeply golden and slightly crusted. Do not rush this step with high heat — slow roasting builds the best colour and flavour.
Check salt, taste for spice, and serve hot. The potatoes should be crispy on the outside with a fluffy interior. Serve as a side with sambar, rasam, and rice.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: potato → raw banana (vazhakkai), peeled and cubed — more traditional and slightly firmer; gingelly-oil → coconut oil as second choice; fennel → cumin seeds — the flavour note shifts but the texture is the same
